audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for forgiveness/release, Son of Humanity authority, Matthew call, table fellowship, mercy not sacrifice, fasting/bridegroom, new wine, hemorrhaging woman, ruler's daughter, blind men, mute man, compassion, harvest imagery.

verses.restored: Entering a boat, Jesus crossed over and came into his own city.
And look, they brought to him a paralyzed man lying on a mat. Seeing their trust, Jesus said to the paralyzed man, Take courage, child; your sins/misalignments are released.
And look, some of the scribes said among themselves, This one blasphemes.
Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said, Why do you think harmful things in your hearts?
For which is easier: to say, Your sins/misalignments are released, or to say, Rise and walk?
But so you may know that the Son of Humanity has authority on earth to release sins/misalignments, then he said to the paralyzed man, Rise, take up your mat, and go to your house.
He rose and went to his house.
When the crowds saw, they were afraid and glorified the Divine, who had given such authority to humans.
Passing on from there, Jesus saw a man called Matthew sitting at the tax office, and he said to him, Follow me. And he rose and followed him.
As he reclined at table in the house, look, many tax collectors and sinners came and reclined with Jesus and his disciples.
When the Pharisees saw, they said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?
Hearing this, he said, Those who are strong have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
Go and learn what this means: I desire mercy and not sacrifice. For I did not come to call the righteous, but sinners/misaligned ones.
Then John's disciples came to him, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?
Jesus said to them, Can the sons of the bridal chamber mourn while the bridegroom is with them? Days will come when the bridegroom is taken from them, and then they will fast.
No one puts a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, for the patch pulls away from the garment, and a worse tear happens.
Nor do they put new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the skins burst, the wine pours out, and the skins are ruined. But they put new wine into fresh wineskins, and both are preserved.
While he was saying these things, look, a ruler came, bowed before him, and said, My daughter has just died; but come lay your hand on her, and she will live.
Jesus rose and followed him, and so did his disciples.
And look, a woman suffering a flow of blood for twelve years came behind him and touched the fringe of his garment.
For she said within herself, If only I touch his garment, I will be saved/healed.
Jesus turned, saw her, and said, Take courage, daughter; your trust has saved/healed you. And the woman was healed from that hour.
When Jesus came into the ruler's house and saw the flute players and the crowd making noise,
he said, Withdraw, for the girl has not died but sleeps. And they laughed at him.
When the crowd had been put outside, he went in, took her hand, and the girl rose.
This report went out into all that land.
As Jesus passed on from there, two blind men followed him, crying out, Have mercy on us, Son of David.
When he entered the house, the blind men came to him, and Jesus said to them, Do you trust that I am able to do this? They said to him, Yes, Lord.
Then he touched their eyes, saying, Let it happen for you according to your trust.
Their eyes were opened. Jesus sternly warned them, saying, See that no one knows.
But they went out and spread his fame in that whole land.
As they were going out, look, they brought to him a mute man troubled by an unclean spirit.
When the spirit was cast out, the mute man spoke. The crowds marveled, saying, Never has anything like this appeared in Israel.
But the Pharisees said, By the ruler of the unclean spirits he casts out the spirits.
Jesus went around all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the good message of the reign, and healing every disease and every weakness.
Seeing the crowds, he was moved with compassion for them, because they were harassed and thrown down, like sheep without a shepherd.
Then he said to his disciples, The harvest is great, but the workers are few.
Therefore ask the Lord of the harvest to send out workers into his harvest.
